info.title: agentsfleet Control Plane API
info.description: API for managing workspaces, fleets, triggers, and runs.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: System status and readiness probes

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Command Line Interface (CLI) sign-in sessions and GitHub sign-in

tags.name: Workspaces
tags.description: Create and manage workspaces

tags.name: Tenant
tags.description: Manage tenant provider settings and models

tags.name: auth-identity-events
tags.description: Process signed account events from Clerk. This route is not for customer fleets.

tags.name: Fleets
tags.description: Create, configure, and run fleets

tags.name: Memory
tags.description: Read and search saved fleet memory

tags.name: Secrets
tags.description: Manage named secrets for fleets in a workspace

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Manage shared provider keys and models

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Read tenant balances and charge records

tags.name: Integration Grants
tags.description: Manage fleet access to third-party services

tags.name: Connectors
tags.description: Connect workspaces to third-party providers

tags.name: Fleet Keys
tags.description: Manage keys that let external clients call a fleet

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Manage tenant admin API keys

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Receive signed events and approval decisions

tags.name: Approvals
tags.description: List, inspect, and resolve pending approvals

tags.name: Fleet
tags.description: Enroll and manage runners

tags.name: Fleet Bundles
tags.description: Manage fleet source bundles in a workspace

tags.name: Fleet library
tags.description: Browse and add reusable fleet sources

tags.name: Model Library
tags.description: List models available to the tenant

get.description: Returns 200 as long as the process is alive and able to serve HTTP. Does not probe dependencies — use /readyz for readiness (database + queue).

commit.description: Git commit SHA of the running build

get.description: Returns Prometheus-format metrics for scraping by monitoring infrastructure.
Returns 200 when the database and event queue are available. Returns 503 when either dependency is unavailable.

503.description: At least one dependency is degraded; ready=false and the failing dependency's boolean is false

get.description: Lists all configured platform-wide LLM provider keys. These are shared keys that workspaces fall back to when no self-managed key is configured.

put.description: Configures a platform-wide LLM provider key by referencing a workspace that has a self-managed key stored. The platform key proxies through the referenced workspace's credential.

source_workspace_id.description: Workspace whose self-managed key to use as the platform key

delete.description: Removes a platform-wide LLM provider key. Workspaces without self-managed keys will no longer be able to use this provider.

get.description: Returns every shared model, ordered by provider and model identifier. Requires the `model:read` scope.

post.summary: Add a priced model to the catalogue
post.description: Creates one catalogue row. `(provider, model_id)` is the row's immutable identity — change either by deleting and re-adding. The rate cache is repopulated on success, so a new price is live with no restart. Requires the `model:admin` scope.

409.description: A catalogue row for this provider and model already exists (`UZ-PROVIDER-008`).

patch.summary: Update a catalogue row's caps and rates
patch.description: Rates-only change; `provider` and `model_id` are the row identity and are immutable on this endpoint. The rate cache is repopulated on success. Requires the `model:admin` scope.

404.description: No catalogue model matches this uid (`UZ-PROVIDER-006`).

delete.description: Refuses to remove the active default model. Choose another default first. Requires the `model:admin` scope.

404.description: No catalogue model matches this uid (`UZ-PROVIDER-006`).

409.description: This model is the active platform default; repoint the default before deleting it (`UZ-PROVIDER-007`).

post.description:
Starts a command-line sign-in session that lasts 5 minutes. The response includes a URL for approval in a browser.

No access token is required. Rate limits may return 429.

public_key.description:
Base64url-encoded P-256 SubjectPublicKeyInfo. The CLI's
ephemeral ECDH public key — used by the dashboard to
derive the AES-256-GCM session key that wraps the JWT.

token_name.description:
Operator-facing label for the resulting credential
(e.g. `macos-cli`, `linux-cli`, `windows-cli`).
Printable ASCII; appears in audit events and
`agentsfleet auth status`.

session_id.description: UUIDv7 session identifier for approval and verification endpoints.

login_url.description: Browser approval URL for this login session.

get.description:
Returns the current sign-in status. No access token is required.

Expired, used, or cancelled sessions return 410 with a stable error code.

200.description: Session is in an active (non-terminal) state

status.description:
`pending` — created, awaiting dashboard approval.
`verification_pending` — dashboard approved, awaiting CLI
verification code.

cli_public_key.description: Echo of the CLI's ECDH public key (base64url P-256 SPKI).

token_name.description: Operator label that the dashboard shows to the user during approval.

expires_at_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds. Session TTL is 5 minutes from creation.

401.description: Session expired (`UZ-AUTH-006`) — the 5-minute TTL elapsed before this poll.

delete.summary: Explicit cancel of a single login session
delete.description:
Used by the dashboard's "cancel this login" button and by the future
sessions surface. Transitions the named session to `aborted` with
`reason="explicit_cancel"`. The Clerk JWT MUST match the session's
`clerk_user_id` (set on `PATCH /v1/auth/sessions/{id}/approve`);
otherwise 403.

patch.description:
Approves a pending command-line sign-in session. The dashboard sends encrypted sign-in data and a six-digit verification code.

A second approval for the same session returns 409 `UZ-AUTH-015`.

dashboard_public_key.description: Temporary public key used to encrypt the sign-in data.

nonce.description: Random 12-byte value used for this encrypted message.

verification_code.description: Exactly six digits shown to the user.

200.description: Session is ready for code verification.

post.description:
Checks the six-digit code and returns encrypted sign-in data. The code can be used once.

A retry from the same client within 60 seconds returns the same response. Later retries return 410 `UZ-AUTH-012`.

`UZ-AUTH-011` means the code did not match. The fifth failed attempt ends the session.
`UZ-AUTH-018` means the code was not six digits. `UZ-AUTH-013` means the session ended.
`UZ-AUTH-014` means approval is pending. `UZ-AUTH-006` means the session expired after 5 minutes.

verification_code.description: The 6-digit code the dashboard displayed to the user.

200.description: Verification succeeded. The response contains encrypted sign-in data.

dashboard_public_key.description: Temporary public key from the approval request.

nonce.description: Random 12-byte value used for this encrypted message.

delete.summary: Bulk-abort every in-flight login session for the caller
delete.description:
Caller-scoped bulk delete. Enumerates every session with the caller's
`clerk_user_id` whose status is `pending` or `verification_pending`
and transitions each to `aborted` with `reason="explicit_cancel"`.

Does NOT revoke already-minted JWTs — Clerk revocation is a separate
problem; an active CLI continues to work until its short-lived JWT
expires. This endpoint clears in-flight login sessions only.

200.description: Sessions aborted (count includes 0 when none were pending)

aborted_count.description: Number of in-flight sessions transitioned to aborted

get.summary: List or search memory entries for a fleet
get.description: Returns memory entries for the fleet. Without `query` this lists most-recent entries (optionally filtered by `category`). With `query`, performs case-insensitive LIKE search across both `key` and `content` (LIKE metacharacters in `query` are escaped — a query of `%` does NOT return every entry).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Fuzzy text search across key and content. When omitted, the endpoint lists most-recent entries.

delete.description:
Deletes one memory entry for this fleet. The key is scoped to the fleet, so a key from another fleet is not deleted.

Missing keys return 404 so an operator can tell a mistyped key from a successful forget.

404.description: No memory entry with that key exists for this fleet.

post.description:
Receives signed account events from Clerk. A `user.created` event creates the user's account and default workspace.

Repeated events return `created:false`. Unsupported event types return 200 with `status: ignored`.

parameters.name: svix-id
parameters.description: Svix message identifier (used for deduplication and signature binding).

parameters.name: svix-timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of the Svix delivery (used for replay protection).

parameters.name: svix-signature
parameters.description: Space-separated list of Svix v1 signatures.

type.description: Clerk event type. Only `user.created` triggers a bootstrap; other types return 200 ignored.

data.description:
Clerk user payload. The handler reads `id`, `email_addresses`,
`primary_email_address_id`, `first_name`, and `last_name`; unknown fields are tolerated.

200.description: Handled. Shape depends on event type: user.created → {workspace_id, workspace_name, created}; user.deleted → {deleted}; any other type → {status: ignored, type}.

workspace_id.description: New or existing default workspace identifier.

created.description: `true` on fresh bootstrap; `false` on idempotent replay.

status.description: `ignored` for event types other than user.created/user.deleted.

type.description: Echoed Clerk event type on ignored events.

post.description:
Receives signed events from a connected provider. GitHub is the supported provider.

A matching event starts runs for subscribed fleets. Duplicate events do not start another run.

parameters.name: X-Hub-Signature-256
parameters.description: Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC)-SHA256 of the raw body, prefixed with `sha256=`.

schema.description: A signed GitHub event body. Events other than `ping` require `installation.id` and `repository.full_name`.

200.description: Valid event that required no fleet run.

202.description: Event matched at least one fleet.

429.description: This API instance reached its in-flight request ceiling. Retry after the advertised delay.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Remaining admission capacity, zero for this response.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp in seconds when immediate retry becomes reasonable.

post.description:
Receives a signed Svix event for one fleet. A valid new event returns 202.

A duplicate or an event for a paused fleet returns 200.

parameters.name: fleet_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the fleet receiving the webhook.

parameters.name: svix-id
parameters.description: Svix message identifier (used for deduplication and signature binding).

parameters.name: svix-timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of the Svix delivery (used for replay protection).

parameters.name: svix-signature
parameters.description: Space-separated list of Svix signatures (v1 scheme).

schema.description: Opaque event envelope; upstream issuer (Svix) defines the payload shape per event type.

200.description: Duplicate event or event ignored because the fleet is paused.

202.description: Webhook accepted and queued for processing.

post.summary: Send a signed event to a fleet
post.description:
Sends a signed event to one fleet. Every request requires a valid provider signature.

A new event returns 202. A duplicate within 24 hours returns `status: duplicate`.

A paused fleet returns `ignored: fleet_paused`. Send the event again after resuming the fleet.

parameters.name: fleet_id
parameters.description: Fleet identifier returned by fleet creation or `agentsfleet install --library <LIBRARY_ID>`.

event_id.description: Unique event identifier. Duplicates within 24 hours are ignored.

data.description: Event payload — any JSON object. Passed as-is into the fleet event loop.

200.description: Duplicate event or event ignored because the fleet is paused.

401.description:
Missing connector settings return `UZ-WH-020`. An invalid signature returns `UZ-WH-010`.

A Slack timestamp outside 5 minutes returns `UZ-WH-011`.

404.description: No active fleet is configured for this source (UZ-WH-001).

post.description: Called by a human (via email link or Slack action) to approve or reject a paused fleet. Body is HMAC-signed by the issuer; the signature is verified against the fleet's webhook secret.

action_id.description: The approval gate's action_id to resolve.

post.summary: Approve or reject an integration grant request
post.description: Human-in-the-loop approval webhook for integration grant requests (Slack, GitHub, etc.). HMAC-signed.

nonce.description: Single-use anti-replay nonce issued with the approval request.

post.description:
Verifies the body with the `webhook_secret` field from the workspace's `github` secret.

Reviewable `pull_request` events and failed completed `workflow_run` events create fleet events. A delivery identifier remains reserved for 72 hours after acceptance.

parameters.name: fleet_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the fleet that owns this webhook receiver.

parameters.name: X-GitHub-Delivery
parameters.description: GitHub-issued delivery UUID. Used as the dedupe key.

parameters.name: X-Hub-Signature-256
parameters.description: HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body, prefixed with `sha256=`.

200.description: Duplicate or ignored event. An ignored event does not reserve its delivery identifier.

get.description:
Returns the providers available to the workspace. Each item shows whether the provider is ready and connected.

Requires the `connector:read` scope.

200.description: Available connectors and their current state.

archetype.description: Connection method used by the provider.

configured.description: True when the provider is ready to connect.

connected.description: True when the workspace is connected.

post.description:
Returns a URL for connecting the workspace to the provider. Open the URL in a browser.

An unknown provider returns 404 `UZ-CONN-004`. An unavailable provider returns 503 `UZ-CONN-001`.

install_url.description: URL to open in the user's browser.

get.description:
Returns `connected` or `not_connected` for one provider. Requires the `connector:read` scope.

An unknown provider returns 404 `UZ-CONN-004`.

team.description: Connected Slack workspace name. Null for other providers.

get.description:
The provider sends the user's browser here after approval. A valid callback connects the workspace and returns the browser to the dashboard.

Invalid, expired, or reused state returns 400 `UZ-CONN-002`. A provider timeout returns `UZ-CONN-003`.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: One-time value supplied through the connection URL.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Authorization code supplied by the provider when required.

parameters.name: installation_id
parameters.description: Installation identifier supplied by providers such as GitHub.

302.description: Connected. The browser returns to the dashboard.

post.description:
Slack sends signed events to this route. Users do not call this route.

An invalid signature returns 401 `UZ-SLK-010`. A timestamp outside 5 minutes returns 401 `UZ-SLK-011`.

parameters.name: X-Slack-Signature
parameters.description: Slack request signature. Slack supplies this value.

parameters.name: X-Slack-Request-Timestamp
parameters.description: Delivery time in Unix seconds. Values outside 5 minutes are rejected.

schema.description: Slack event or URL verification request.

200.description: Accepted, duplicate, verification, or ignored response.

challenge.description: Value returned for Slack URL verification.

post.description: Creates a workspace in the caller's tenant. An empty body uses a server-assigned name.

name.description: Human-readable workspace name. Must be unique within the tenant. If omitted, the server assigns a Heroku-style name (e.g. `jolly-harbor-482`).
Human-readable workspace name. Always present — caller-supplied or server-generated Heroku-style.

get.description: Returns the balance shared by every workspace in the tenant. The response also shows whether the balance is empty and whether a free trial is active.

updated_at.description: Last update time in Unix milliseconds.

is_exhausted.description: True after a charge reduces the balance to zero.

exhausted_at.description: Time of the first zero balance in Unix milliseconds. Null until then.

active.description: True while the free trial is active.

ends_at_ms.description: Free-trial end time in Unix milliseconds.

get.description: Returns charge records with the newest record first. Use `limit` and `cursor` to read more records.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of records to return. The default is 50. Valid values are 1 through 200.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Token from `next_cursor` in the previous response. Omit it for the first page.

200.description: Tenant charge records with the newest record first.

next_cursor.description: Token for the next page. Null when no records remain.

get.description: Returns the detail records for one charge, oldest first. An unknown `event_id` returns an empty `items` list.

200.description: Charge details for the requested event.

get.summary: List every workspace the caller's tenant owns
get.description: Returns the full set of workspaces owned by the caller's tenant. Backs the dashboard workspace switcher: a user can switch to any workspace in this list without re-issuing the JWT, because workspace authorisation is tenant-scoped. No pagination — a tenant's workspace count is bounded and small in practice.

name.description: Human-readable workspace name. Nullable — pre-rename workspaces may not have one.

get.description: Returns the tenant's provider mode, provider name, model, context limit, and credential reference. Secret values are never returned.

put.description:
Sets the provider mode and model. Self-managed mode requires a `credential_ref` that names an existing secret.
If the model entry does not exist, this request creates it.

credential_ref.description: User-named vault key. Required when mode=self_managed.

model.description: Optional model override. Falls back to the credential's stored model when absent.

delete.summary: Reset tenant LLM provider to platform default
delete.description: Equivalent to `PUT {mode: "platform"}`. Writes the explicit mode=platform row so the dashboard can distinguish "never configured" from "explicitly reset".

get.description: One entry per configured model; two entries can share a `secret_ref`. Each entry is joined to its secret's non-secret metadata (provider, kind, base_url, has_key) — `api_key` is never serialised. `active` is computed against the tenant's current provider selection (`GET /v1/tenants/me/provider`). Before listing, a pre-registry activation with no matching entry is synthesized idempotently, so the registry self-heals with no separate backfill.

post.description: Creates one `(model_id, secret_ref)` row. `secret_ref` must already name a vault secret in the tenant's primary workspace — store or reuse one via `/v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/secrets` first. Does not activate the entry; activate via `PUT /v1/tenants/me/provider`.

secret_ref.description: User-named vault secret; may already back other entries.

404.description: secret_ref does not name a vault secret (`UZ-MODELS-002`).

409.description: An entry with this exact (model_id, secret_ref) pair already exists (`UZ-MODELS-003`).

patch.description: Model-only change; `secret_ref` is immutable on this endpoint — create a new entry to point at a different secret.

404.description: No entry with this id for the calling tenant (`UZ-MODELS-004`).

409.description: The new (model_id, secret_ref) pair collides with an existing entry (`UZ-MODELS-003`).

delete.description: Idempotent — deleting an id that doesn't exist (already removed, or never existed) still returns 204. The referenced vault secret is never touched; sibling entries sharing it survive.

409.description: This entry is the tenant's active selection — switch to another entry first (`UZ-MODELS-001`).

get.summary: List secrets stored for a workspace
get.description: Returns secret names and non-secret details. Secret values are never returned.

post.description:
Stores an encrypted JSON object. Secret values are never returned.

`data` must be a non-empty object no larger than 4 KiB. Larger values return `UZ-VAULT-002`.

Strings, arrays, scalars, and empty objects return `UZ-VAULT-001`.

name.description: Secret identifier a Fleet interpolates as `${secrets.<name>.<field>}`.

data.description:
JSON object whose top-level keys are the field names a skill
references via `${secrets.<name>.<field>}`. The runtime never
inspects the schema — this is opaque to the platform.

patch.description:
Replaces only the stored `api_key` field. Other fields keep their current values.

A missing secret returns `UZ-VAULT-003`.

api_key.description: The new key. Rejected empty or over the 4 KiB stored-object limit (`UZ-VAULT-002`), and never echoed back or logged.

delete.summary: Delete a secret from the workspace vault
delete.description: Idempotent — returns 204 whether or not the secret existed.

post.description: Creates a tenant admin key with the `agt_t` prefix. The raw key is returned once. Only its SHA-256 hash is saved.

key_name.description: Unique per tenant. Alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores only.

key.description: Raw `agt_t` tenant key (70 chars). Shown once — store securely.

409.description: Key name already exists for this tenant (`UZ-APIKEY-005`).

get.description: Returns metadata for every tenant API key visible to the caller. Results are always scoped to the caller's tenant; `key_hash` is never returned.

total.description: Total rows across all pages for this tenant.

patch.description: Partial lifecycle update. Body must be `{"active": false}` — re-activation is not supported; mint a new key instead. Sets `revoked_at` and `updated_at`.

409.description: Key is already revoked (`UZ-APIKEY-006`) or attempted to set `active: true` (`UZ-APIKEY-007`).

delete.summary: Delete a revoked tenant API key
delete.description: Deletes a revoked tenant API key. An active key returns 409 `UZ-APIKEY-008`.

409.description: Active key must be revoked before deletion (`UZ-APIKEY-008`).

get.summary: List fleet keys in a workspace
get.description: Returns fleet-key metadata only — never returns `key_hash`.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

post.summary: Issue a Fleet key for a Fleet
post.description: Creates a fleet key with the `agt_a` prefix. The raw key is returned once. Only its SHA-256 hash is saved.

key.description: Raw `agt_a` API key. Shown once — store securely.

delete.description: Revokes the Fleet key immediately. Subsequent requests with the key receive 401.

get.description: Returns visible fleets with the newest fleet first. Omit `cursor` for the first page. Use the returned cursor to read the next page.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque page cursor from a prior response's `cursor` field, formatted `{created_at_epoch_ms}:{fleet_id}`. Omit for the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Defaults to 20. Clamped to a maximum of 100.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

cursor.description: Opaque cursor to fetch the next page. `null` when there are no more pages.

post.description:
Creates a fleet from one library entry. Use either `platform_library_id` or `tenant_library_id`.

The service creates a default API trigger when the library has no trigger.

requestBody.description: Choose one library entry. Use `platform_library_id` for the shared library or `tenant_library_id` for the workspace library.

platform_library_id.description: Slug id of a platform catalog library entry to install.

tenant_library_id.description: UUIDv7 of one of this workspace's tenant library entries to install.

name.description: Optional operator-supplied fleet name (slug-safe, lowercase/digits/hyphen, max 64 chars). Overrides the SKILL.md-derived name so one library entry can back multiple fleets in a workspace, each with its own name + webhooks. Absent ⇒ the SKILL.md `name:`.
Fleet name parsed from TRIGGER.md frontmatter or the generated default trigger — the canonical identifier the user will see in `agentsfleet status` and CLI output.

webhook_urls.description: Map of each webhook trigger's source → its delivery URL (`{api_url}/v1/webhooks/{fleet_id}/{source}`). Empty object when the fleet declares no webhook triggers.

get.summary: List the platform Fleet library catalog
get.description: Returns the first-party (platform) Fleet library catalog. Rows contain only metadata and requirement hints; they carry no credential values or object-store keys. The workspace gallery union lives at GET /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/fleet-libraries.

get.summary: List the platform Fleet library catalog
get.description: Lists every entry in the global platform catalog. Published, draft, and entries whose bundle was never fetched all appear. Unlike the workspace gallery, this operator view hides nothing: it shows what is live and what still needs work. Requires the `platform-library:write` scope. Metadata only — never bundle markdown, a support-file body, or an object-store key. Each row carries an `etag` that an editor can send as `If-Match` on PATCH.

post.summary: Onboard a platform Fleet library entry
post.description: Onboards a Fleet library entry into the global platform catalog from a GitHub source reference. Requires the `platform-library:write` scope. The canonical bundle is written to internal object storage keyed by content hash; the response carries metadata only — never an object-store key or support-file content.

201.description: Entry onboarded into the platform catalog.

409.description: The bundle's name is a catalog id already owned by a different repository; retry with `replace` (`UZ-CATALOG-004`).

patch.summary: Curate, publish, or unpublish a platform Fleet library entry

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The catalog id — the bundle's SKILL.md frontmatter name (a slug, not a UUID).

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description: Optional catalog row version from the list response. Stale values return 412 with the current `etag`.

patch.description: Partial update. `description` and `required_credentials_reasons` are the two fields no bundle can supply, so they are operator-owned: a later bundle refetch never overwrites them. `published` moves the entry between `draft` (stored, invisible to every tenant) and `public` (live in every workspace gallery and installable). Publishing an entry whose bundle was never fetched is refused — a published entry always has something to install. Requires the `platform-library:write` scope. Send `If-Match` with the row's `etag` to reject stale edits before they can repoint the source or unpublish the entry. Omitting the header preserves last-write-wins behavior.

200.description: The entry as it now stands.

409.description: Publish was requested for an entry with no bundle; fetch it from the repository first (`UZ-CATALOG-002`).

412.description: The `If-Match` value is stale. The problem body carries the current `etag` (`UZ-CATALOG-005`).

delete.summary: Delete an unpublished platform Fleet library entry

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The catalog id — the bundle's SKILL.md frontmatter name (a slug, not a UUID).

delete.description: Removes a catalog entry. Only an entry that is NOT published may be deleted. A live fleet is never taken away from the tenants who can install it, so unpublish it first. Workspaces that already installed the fleet are unaffected: an install snapshots the bundle, so it keeps running. Requires the `platform-library:write` scope.

409.description: The entry is published; unpublish it first, then delete (`UZ-CATALOG-003`).

get.summary: List the workspace Fleet library gallery
get.description: Returns the union of the platform Fleet library catalog and this workspace's own tenant entries — and nothing from another workspace. Each entry carries identity, source, requirements, and support-file summaries; never an object-store key.

post.summary: Onboard a tenant Fleet library entry
post.description: Onboards a Fleet library entry into the caller's workspace catalog from a GitHub source reference. Requires the `library:write` scope plus ownership of the target workspace. Re-onboarding identical bytes converges on one `(workspace_id, content_hash)` row. The response carries metadata only.

201.description: Entry onboarded into the workspace catalog.

get.description:
Returns one fleet's editable source, trigger markdown, bundle pin, trigger list, status, and lifetime counters.

The response carries an `ETag` header over `source_markdown` and `trigger_markdown`. Send that value as `If-Match` when saving source changes to avoid overwriting another operator's edit.

ETag.description: Version of the editable source fields.

404.description: No fleet with that id exists in this workspace. Cross-workspace reads also return 404.

patch.description:
Updates fleet files or status. Every request field is optional.

`config_json` and `trigger_markdown` cannot be used together. A killed fleet cannot be changed.

Source editors can send `If-Match` with the `ETag` from GET. A stale tag returns 412 with the current tag in the problem body; omitting the header preserves last-write-wins behavior.

Status changes require an operator role. Config changes require workspace membership.

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description: Optional source-version tag from GET. Stale values return 412 with the current `etag`.

config_json.description:
Replaces the fleet config with a JSON string.
Mutually exclusive with `trigger_markdown`.

trigger_markdown.description:
Replaces `TRIGGER.md` and updates the fleet name and triggers.
Must be non-empty. Mutually exclusive with `config_json`.

source_markdown.description:
Replaces `SKILL.md` and its settings. Must be non-empty.
The skill name must match the current fleet name.

status.description:
New fleet state. `active` resumes, and `stopped` can resume later.
`killed` is final. Only the service can set `paused`.

ETag.description: Fresh version of the editable source fields after the update.

status.description: Present only when the request body included a `status` field. Echoes the new status.

etag.description: Fresh version of the editable source fields after the update.

412.description: The `If-Match` value is stale. The problem body carries the current `etag`.

delete.description:
Permanently deletes the fleet, events, runs, approvals, grants, keys, and memory. This cannot be undone.

Set the fleet status to `killed` first. Otherwise the request returns 409.

Requires an operator role.

get.summary: List integration grants for a fleet
get.description: Returns pending, approved, and revoked grants, newest first.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

delete.description: Immediately revokes a grant. A revoked grant blocks the fleet on its next call against the affected service.

post.description: Called by a fleet (or on its behalf) to request permission to use a third-party integration (e.g. Slack, Gmail). Creates a pending grant that a human approves via the grant-approval webhook.

200.description: Existing grant returned (idempotent — a pending or approved grant for this service already existed).

post.summary: Post a chat message to a fleet
post.description: Starts a fleet run with a chat event. Returns an event identifier for tracking in the activity stream.

message.description: Operator message body. Up to 8192 bytes. The server wraps it in a JSON object with a "message" field as the envelope's request payload.

202.description: Event enqueued. The CLI uses event_id to filter SSE frames.

409.description: Fleet is not active (`UZ-AGT-012`). Resume with `agentsfleet resume <FLEET>`, then retry.

get.description: Returns fleet events with the newest event first. Filter with `actor` or `since`. You cannot use `since` and `cursor` together.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque base64url-no-pad cursor returned in `next_cursor`. Mutually exclusive with `since`.

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description: Glob filter against the row's actor. `*` is a wildcard. Examples - 'steer:kishore', 'webhook:github', 'webhook:*'.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound on `created_at`. Accepts Go-style durations (15s/30m/2h/7d) or RFC 3339 timestamps. Mutually exclusive with `cursor`.

get.description:
Opens a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream for new fleet activity. Each message includes `id`, `event`, and `data`.

Identifiers restart at 0 for each connection. The route ignores `Last-Event-ID`.

At capacity, the route returns 503 `UZ-API-002` with `Retry-After`. Read missed events before reconnecting.

get.summary: List a workspace's aggregate event history
get.description: Cursor-paginated list of operator-visible events across every fleet in the workspace, newest first. Replaces the deleted activity endpoint. Optional `fleet_id` filter narrows to one fleet. Same query shape as the per-fleet endpoint - `actor` glob, `since=` (duration or RFC 3339), and a `cursor` mutually exclusive with `since`.

parameters.name: fleet_id
parameters.description: Drill down to one fleet within the workspace.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a prior response. Mutually exclusive with `since`.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound on `created_at`. Go-style duration or RFC 3339 timestamp.

get.summary: Read the workspace's onboarding checklist state
get.description: Returns every signal the Getting Started checklist needs in one call. Five signals are derived server-side in a single query. A model is configured, a fleet exists, a credential exists, an event has been processed, and a steer event exists. Three more are the caller's stored UI preferences: dismissed, collapsed, and CLI ticked. This replaces six separate requests with one.

200.description: The onboarding checklist state for the calling user

get.summary: Read the caller's dashboard preferences for a workspace
get.description: Returns every dashboard preference the calling user has set in this workspace, as an object keyed by preference key. A user who has set nothing gets `{"prefs": {}}` - never a 404, so a client can tell "no preferences" from "preferences unavailable" without branching. Preferences are per user AND per workspace: a second workspace starts its onboarding checklist fresh.

200.description: The caller's preference bag for this workspace

prefs.description: Preference key to value. Values are client-owned and opaque to the server. Absent keys have never been set.

put.description: Upserts a single preference for the calling user in this workspace and returns the full updated bag. The request body IS the value - any well-formed JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) value up to 1 KiB, stored verbatim and never interpreted by the server. `pref_key` must be one the dashboard declares; anything else is refused with `UZ-PREFS-001` and no row is written. Concurrent writes to one key are last-write-wins by design: a preference is a single toggle, so a lost write costs one click.

parameters.name: pref_key
parameters.description: The preference to write. Must be a key the dashboard declares.

requestBody.description: The preference value. Any JSON value up to 1 KiB.

200.description: The caller's preference bag, including the written key

get.summary: List pending approval gates for a workspace
get.description:
Returns approval gates oldest-first (oldest is most urgent). Each row
surfaces the fleet's proposed action, gathered evidence, blast-radius
assessment, and timeout countdown. Filter by fleet, gate kind, or
status. Cursor pagination over (requested_at, id) so concurrent
inserts don't cause silent skips.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Defaults to "pending". Supply "approved", "denied", "timed_out" to query terminal states.

get.summary: Get a single approval gate by id
get.description: Drives the dashboard detail page. 404 when the gate doesn't exist or belongs to a different workspace.

post.description:
Approves a pending request. If two callers resolve it, the first result wins.

Other callers receive 409 with the existing result and resolver.

Denies a pending request. The proposed tool call does not run.

If two callers resolve the request, the first result wins.

Enrolls a runner into the fleet. Authed by an existing operator credential (Clerk JWT or `agt_t` API key with admin role) — there is no enrollment token. Mints a durable `agt_r` runner token (returned once) and stores only its SHA-256 hash.

201.description: Runner registered; the bearer token is returned exactly once.

get.description: Platform-admin operator-plane read of the whole fleet. Paginated, read-only. Each row carries a derived `liveness` — never the stored auth state, never the token hash.

patch.description: Platform-admin operator action on a single runner. `cordon` → cordoned, `drain` → draining, `revoke` → revoked (terminal; a revoked runner cannot transition back).

get.description: Platform-admin read of a single runner's append-only history. Paginated; optionally filtered by event type and an occurred-at millisecond window.

parameters.name: event_type
parameters.description: Filter to a single runner event type.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound on occurred_at, epoch milliseconds (inclusive).

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Upper bound on occurred_at, epoch milliseconds (inclusive). Must be >= since.

get.description: Returns models available to the tenant. Each item includes its provider, context limit, and usage fields. An empty library returns an empty array.

version.description: Date of the most recently updated model, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD in UTC.

id.description: The model_id. Provider hosting is encoded in the id itself.

BearerAuth.description: Obtain a token via the CLI auth flow (POST /v1/auth/sessions) or GitHub OAuth

docs_uri.description: Stable link to documentation for this error code

title.description: Short human-readable label, same for every occurrence of this code

detail.description: Instance-specific context describing what went wrong
detail.example: No fleet with id 'abc123' in this workspace.

current_state.description: Resource state that blocked the requested change. Present only on 409 responses.

user_message.description:
Plain message safe to show to a user. Present only when an error defines one.

Use `detail` or `title` when this field is absent.
user_message.example: We couldn't find that Fleet. It may have been deleted, or the ID doesn't match one in this workspace.

etag.description: Resource version returned on 412 responses so the caller can refetch and retry.

ReadyzBody.description: Response body for GET /readyz. Identical shape for both 200 (healthy) and 503 (degraded) responses — only the boolean values differ.

ready.description: Overall readiness — true only when every dependency is healthy. Mirrors the HTTP status (true ↔ 200, false ↔ 503).

database.description: True when the database accepts a test query.

queue.description: True when the event queue is available.

PlatformKey.description: A platform-wide LLM provider key configuration

source_workspace_id.description: Workspace whose self-managed key is used

model.description: The priced (provider, model_id) this default resolves to. Non-null for the active row; NULL for inactive rows.

active.description: Whether this platform key is active

updated_at.description: Epoch milliseconds of the last update.

context_cap_tokens.description: Hard input-token cap for the model. Must be > 0.

input_nanos_per_mtok.description: Input-token price in nanos per 1M tokens.

cached_input_nanos_per_mtok.description: Cached-input-token price in nanos per 1M tokens.

output_nanos_per_mtok.description: Output-token price in nanos per 1M tokens.

uid.description: UUIDv7 row key. Used in the URL because `model_id` may contain `/`.

has_key.description: Whether the referenced secret's body carries a non-empty api_key.

context_cap_tokens.description: From the model-caps catalogue when the provider/model is known; omitted otherwise.

input_nanos_per_mtok.description: Input-token price in nanos per 1M tokens when the model is in the platform library.

cached_input_nanos_per_mtok.description: Cached-input-token price in nanos per 1M tokens when available.

output_nanos_per_mtok.description: Output-token price in nanos per 1M tokens when the model is in the platform library.

active.description: True when (secret_ref, model_id) matches the tenant's current provider selection.

platform_default_available.description: True when a shared default model is available.

platform_default.description: The active platform default's identity — present exactly when `platform_default_available` is true (both derive from the same read). Lets the Models page render the Default row's model, provider, and context without a second request.

SecretSummary.description:
A workspace secret's non-secret descriptors — the `api_key`/`data`
payload is never projected here.

kind.description:
Server-derived classification from the stored body's `provider`
field — the client reads this, it never re-guesses from the name.
`custom_secret` also covers legacy/corrupt bodies that fail to
decrypt or parse, so listing always returns 200.

TenantApiKey.description: Metadata for a tenant API key. `key_hash` and the raw key are never returned.

last_used_at.description: Null until the key is used successfully for API-key authentication.

FleetTrigger.description: A single trigger projected into the fleet list row.

triggers.description: Configured fleet triggers. Null when no triggers are set.

events_processed.description: Lifetime count of events processed by this fleet.

budget_used_nanos.description: Total balance used by this fleet, in nanos.

required_credentials_reasons.description: Per-credential display-only "why this fleet needs it" copy, keyed by credential name (e.g. {"github": "review your pull requests"}). An install-gate hint, NOT a security control — credential validation reads required_credentials. Optional: older catalogs may omit it.

FleetLibraryCatalogEntry.description: One row of the platform Fleet library catalog, as the operator sees it. Metadata only — never bundle markdown, a support-file body, or an object-store key.

id.description: The catalog id — the bundle's SKILL.md frontmatter name.

description.description: Operator-owned after the entry is created. Seeded from the bundle on the first fetch; a later refetch never overwrites it.

source_repo.description: The GitHub repository the bundle was fetched from, as `owner/repo`.

source_ref.description: The git ref the bundle was fetched at.

visibility.description: The publish lifecycle. `draft` — the bundle is stored and the fleet is invisible to every tenant. `public` — live in every workspace gallery and installable.

content_hash.description: The content hash of the stored bundle, or null when no bundle has ever been fetched for this entry. A null hash can never be published.

required_credentials_reasons.description: Per-credential "why this fleet needs it" copy, keyed by credential name, shown at the install gate. Operator-owned: the importer cannot derive it, and a bundle refetch never overwrites it.

etag.description: Optimistic-concurrency tag over the operator-editable catalog row fields. Send it as `If-Match` on PATCH to prevent stale overwrites.

source_kind.description: Where the entry comes from. `github` and `template` are fetched and validated server-side; `upload` carries the SKILL.md (and optional TRIGGER.md) Markdown inline for a skill-only entry.

replace.description: Platform tier only. The catalog id comes from the bundle's SKILL.md frontmatter name, not this repository path. So a bundle may declare a name another repository already owns. That is refused with `UZ-CATALOG-004`, rather than silently swapping the bundle every workspace installs. Retry with `replace: true` to overwrite it deliberately.

source_ref.description: Required for `github` (an `owner/repo` locator) and `template` (a first-party template id).

ref.description: Optional branch or tag for `github` sources; absent fetches the default branch. The dashboard's Fetch-update sends the entry's stored ref, so a ref pinned via the catalog PATCH is honored by the refetch rather than silently reset. Same character rules as a repository segment.

skill_markdown.description: Raw SKILL.md bytes. Required for `upload`; ignored for fetched sources.

trigger_markdown.description: Optional raw TRIGGER.md bytes for `upload`. Ignored for fetched sources.

FleetLibraryOnboardResult.description: Result of onboarding a Fleet library entry (platform or tenant tier). Carries the entry identity, its tier in `visibility`, the content hash, the derived requirements, and support-file summaries — never an object-store key or support-file content.

id.description: Entry id — the slug for platform entries, a UUIDv7 for tenant entries.

visibility.description: The catalog tier the entry was onboarded into.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 content hash of the onboarded snapshot.

FleetLibraryCatalogPatch.description:
Partial update of a catalog entry. Every field is optional, and an absent field is left untouched. Editing the description never blanks the credential copy, and the entry id is never patchable.

A `source_repo` or `source_ref` that differs from the stored value discards the stored bundle and returns the entry to draft, atomically. The bundle was built from the old source, so an entry never advertises a source it is not serving. Re-sending the stored value is a no-op.

name.description: Display name. Operator-owned — survives bundle refetches.

source_repo.description: `owner/repo`. Validated with the same rules as onboarding.

source_ref.description: Branch or tag the next fetch should use.

published.description: true publishes the entry (requires a bundle); false withdraws it back to draft, which removes it from every gallery and blocks new installs. Workspaces already running the fleet are unaffected.

FleetLibraryGalleryEntry.description: One gallery row — a platform or tenant Fleet library entry. Carries identity, description, source, derived requirements, per-credential reason copy, and support-file summaries; never an object-store key or support-file content.

description.description: The entry's SKILL.md description, rendered on the gallery card. Platform rows carry their curated copy; tenant rows carry the description derived from the onboarded SKILL frontmatter.

visibility.description: The catalog tier this template belongs to.

required_credentials_reasons.description: Display-only {credential_name: reason} map driving the install gate's purpose copy. Platform rows surface their curated reasons; tenant rows surface an empty object (the importer derives no per-credential copy).

source_markdown.description: Current SKILL.md markdown for this fleet.

trigger_markdown.description: Current TRIGGER.md markdown, or null when the fleet has no trigger file.

bundle_content_hash.description: Installed bundle content hash, or null when the fleet was not installed from a bundle.

FleetEvent.description: One fleet event in newest-first history order.

cost_nanos.description: Billed cost for this event in nanos, mapped from the event's `credit_deducted_nanos` charge. Null when no charge has been recorded.

gate_kind.description: Classification driving UI grouping. Empty string when not classified.

proposed_action.description: Human-readable summary of what the fleet wants to do.

evidence.description: Fleet-gathered context (free-form). Rendered as expandable JSON tree.

blast_radius.description: Fleet's assessment of the action's reach.

detail.description: Optional reason text supplied by the resolver.

timeout_at.description: ms epoch — sweeper auto-denies after this point

updated_at.description: Set on terminal transition; doubles as resolved_at.

reason.description: Optional free-form note attached to the resolution.

ApprovalAlreadyResolvedResponse.description: Error response for a request that was already resolved. The response includes the first result, time, and resolver.

RunnerRegisterRequest.description: Self-reported runner identity at enrollment. `sandbox_tier` is telemetry only — placement keys off operator-assigned trust, not this claim.

host_id.description: Operator-supplied host identifier for this runner (1-256 chars).

sandbox_tier.description: Isolation strength the runner self-reports. Stored as telemetry.

RunnerRegisterResponse.description: The durable runner identity plus its bearer token. The `runner_token` is returned exactly once; the server stores only its SHA-256 hash.

runner_token.description: The `agt_r`-prefixed bearer token the runner presents on every later call. Returned once; never retrievable again.

RunnerSummary.description: One fleet row as returned to the operator. `liveness` is derived at read time (never stored); the auth `token_hash` and stored auth state are never returned.

admin_state.description: Operator setting. Only `active` accepts runner requests.

liveness.description: Derived runtime liveness. `registered` = minted, never seen; `busy` = holds a live lease; `online` = heartbeat fresh; `offline` = heartbeat stale.

last_seen_at.description: Epoch milliseconds of the last heartbeat; 0 if never seen.

created_at.description: Epoch milliseconds the runner was registered.

metadata.description: Event-type-specific metadata (e.g. host_id, from/to admin_state). Shape varies by event_type.
